channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: World Leaders Push to Save Iran Talks Amid Israel’s Attacks in Lebanon
item.description: President Trump’s cease-fire with Iran appears at risk as Vice President JD Vance heads to Pakistan for discussions with Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A Lebanese man stood on a pile of debris in the Corniche El Mazraa area of Beirut after the Israeli bombardment of the capital on Wednesday.

item.title: What the Cease-Fire Means for Iran
item.description: Emerging from weeks of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes, an emboldened Iran has 10 demands for talks during the tenuous cease-fire, according to Iranian state media. Our reporter Erika Solomon assesses Iran’s position.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Christina Thornell, David Seekamp and Joey Sendaydiego

item.title: Fallout of War Piles Economic Pain Onto Europe’s Political Stress
item.description: Europe is finding itself on the outs with Russia, China and the U.S., in what’s amounting to its very own “Mean Girls” moment.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Patrick Junker for The New York Times
item.media:description: A liquefied natural gas terminal in Germany. Most of that L.N.G. comes from the United States, underscoring Europe’s vulnerability to American supplies.

item.title: Iran Unable to Find Mines It Planted in Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Says
item.description: The lost mines have prevented Iran from quickly complying with President Trump’s demand to allow more ships to pass through the waterway.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships near the Strait of Hormuz, as seen last month from northern Ras al-Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Artemis II Splashdown Gives NASA Momentum in Renewed Moon Race
item.description: The astronauts — three Americans and one Canadian — captivated the world with their historic mission.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang
item.media:credit: Bill Ingalls/NASA, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Splashing down in the Pacific Ocean on Saturday near San Diego, the Artemis II mission came to a successful conclusion.

item.title: See Photos From All 10 Days of NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission
item.description: Over 10 days, the astronauts of NASA’s lunar flyby mission have achieved the near impossible.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Eric Swalwell Faces Sex Assault Accusation as He Runs for California Governor
item.description: Eric Swalwell, a Democratic congressman, denied the accounts published by CNN and The San Francisco Chronicle. Nancy Pelosi was among those who said he should withdraw from the California governor’s race.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Campaign officials and supporters are abandoning Representative Eric Swalwell.

item.title: Trump Is Racing to Redefine ‘America First’ in a Time of War
item.description: Washington has been debating the thrust of Mr. Trump’s foreign policy for more than a decade, with the only consensus being that “America First” means whatever the president says it does.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump holding a “Make America Great Again” hat on his way to his golf club in Miami last year.

item.title: Officials Release Design for 250-Foot Arch in Washington, as Trump Seeks Another Imprint
item.description: The president has proposed the arch, which would rise on a Washington roundabout across from the Lincoln Memorial, as a way to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:description: A rendering released by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts of plans for a 250-foot triumphal arch in Washington.

item.title: F.A.A. Says Military Can Use Anti-Drone Lasers in U.S. Airspace
item.description: An interagency dispute over the use of high-energy lasers near airports led the Federal Aviation Administration to temporarily close the airspace twice in Texas this year.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: Laser (Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation)
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Locust, an anti-drone laser system that has been used recently along the border with Mexico, last month in Albuquerque, N.M.

item.title: How Lu Xun, a Famous Chinese Writer, Became a Cute Communist Mascot
item.description: The Chinese Communist Party has turned Lu Xun, a Mao-era hero who excoriated the establishment, into a bland, Disney-style character.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Qilai Shen
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times

item.title: Trump Says First Lady ‘Had a Right’ to Talk About Epstein
item.description: President Trump said in an interview that he had known his wife wanted to address rumors about the late sex offender at some point, but that he had not known exactly what she would say.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.media:description: Melania Trump before speaking in the Grand Foyer of the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Harris Says She Is ‘Thinking About’ Running for President Again in 2028
item.description: At a convention in New York, possible candidates for the Democratic presidential primary are making overtures to Black voters, a vital constituency.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck and Tim Balk
item.media:description: Kamala Harris, former vice president and the 2024 Democratic presidential candidate, spoke at the National Action Network Convention in New York on Friday.

item.title: Potential Democratic 2028 Candidates Condemn Trump’s ‘War of Choice’ in Iran
item.description: At a convention in New York, several Democrats mulling presidential bids rallied around opposition to President Trump’s decision to attack Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Burned Before, Democrats Abruptly Revive the Idea of Impeaching Trump
item.description: Democrats in Congress have assiduously avoided talk of a third impeachment of President Trump, concerned that it would distract from their midterm campaign message. That tide seems to have turned.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump returning to the White House last month.

item.title: Trump Moves Top Aide James Blair to Political Operation Ahead of Midterms
item.description: Mr. Blair will “lead the charge from the outside,” the president said on social media. The move comes as Republicans are bracing for a potentially difficult November election.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:description: James Blair, a deputy chief of staff, is taking over President Trump’s outside political operation and its $300 million-plus budget, Mr. Trump announced on Friday.

item.title: Can Food Actually Be Medicine? These Doctors Say Yes
item.description: Prescribing produce, crafting meals: More medical schools are teaching students how to cook and use food as a tool for treating patients.
item.dc:creator: Kim Severson
item.media:credit: Tony Luong for The New York Times
item.media:description: Eliza Leone, a dietitian, teaches culinary medicine to a mix of medical, dental and dietetics students from Tufts University.

item.title: The Jump Rope Queen of Beverly Hills
item.description: Annie Judis, 82, gets a kick out of defying expectations about what the body can do.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Friedman
item.media:credit: Cassandra Giraldo for The New York Times

item.title: Eliot Engel, a 16-Term Bronx Liberal in Congress, Dies at 79
item.description: After defeating a Democratic fixture in a 1988 primary, he rose to lead the House Foreign Affairs Committee, then was toppled himself in 2020, undone by an overheard comment.
item.dc:creator: Sam Roberts
item.media:description: Representative Eliot Engel in his Bronx office in 1998. He was a reliable champion of liberal causes.

item.title: A Republican Strategist Explains What’s Going On With the Party
item.description: Without Trump on the midterms ballot, how will his party fare?
item.dc:creator: Liam Donovan and John Guida
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Fareed Zakaria on the Moral Cost of Trump’s War
item.description: The foreign policy analyst Fareed Zakaria explains how the Iran war has been a turning point in America’s standing in the world.
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein and Annie Galvin

item.title: Eric Adams, Citizen of the World, Becomes an Actual Citizen of Albania
item.description: Mr. Adams, the former mayor of New York City, has expressed fondness for Albania, visiting the country in his last weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Eric Adams has long had a soft spot in his heart for a certain Balkan nation.

item.title: Molotov Cocktail Is Hurled at Home of Sam Altman, OpenAI’s CEO
item.description: The device burned an exterior gate of the house in San Francisco, the police said, and it was unclear whether the artificial intelligence executive was home. The authorities arrested a suspect.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs, Kalley Huang and Heather Knight
item.media:description: It was unclear whether Sam Altman was home at the time of the attack.

item.title: Banks Are Warned About Anthropic’s New, Powerful A.I. Technology
item.description: In an unusual move, the Treasury secretary and the Federal Reserve chair gathered bank executives to caution about cyberthreats posed by artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland and Colby Smith
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The stark message was delivered by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent in a hastily called meeting.

item.title: Thomas S. Langner, Who Linked Social Ills to Mental Illness, Dies at 102
item.description: He helped lead what became known as the Midtown Manhattan Study, which showed that mental impairment is highly correlated with low socioeconomic status.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Rosenwald
